styles.label: Brutalist
styles.notes: Raw concrete geometry, exposed structural grid, anti-decoration, monumental type, zero border-radius, visible construction. Think Barbican Centre meets a CLI. Harsh, honest, unapologetically structural.

passVariants.direction: Monolithic slab layout. Everything is a heavy concrete block with raw paper textures and harsh black ink borders. Navigation is a vertical stack of oversized labels set in enormous display-weight headings crushed against tight body text. Color is almost absent apart from one explosive red accent on interactive elements.

styles.label: Super Parallax
styles.notes: Parallax-driven design where multiple depth layers create a rich 3D scrolling experience. Background, midground, and foreground elements move at different speeds. Hero sections have dramatic depth. Content reveals through scroll-triggered parallax animations. Everything feels like looking through layered glass panes.

passVariants.direction: Multi-layer parallax with frosted glass surfaces at different depths. Navigation is a floating top bar that parallax-scrolls at a different rate than content. Cards sit on different z-layers, creating a diorama effect when scrolling. Background uses subtle gradient shifts that respond to scroll position. Heavy use of backdrop-filter blur to separate layers.

passVariants.direction: Cinematic scroll-driven parallax where each view is a full-screen scene. Scrolling between sections feels like a camera dolly through a film set. Large hero images and backgrounds move at 0.3x speed while content moves at 1x. Section transitions use dramatic parallax reveals — content sliding up over parallax backgrounds. Think Apple product pages meets a dashboarding app.

styles.label: Grid Master
styles.notes: Grid-obsessed design where every element aligns to a visible or implied grid system. Swiss design principles meet modern dashboard UX. Strict column/row alignment, visible grid lines as decorative elements, modular scale typography, and mathematical spacing ratios. Maximum information density with total visual order.

passVariants.direction: Swiss International Typographic Style — ultra-precise grid with visible construction lines, Helvetica-style type, asymmetric layouts within a strict grid, and red accent used sparingly. Navigation uses a compact grid-aligned toolbar. Content modules snap to a 12-column grid with mathematical gutters. Zero decorative elements — pure information design.

styles.label: Vertical-Horizontal
styles.notes: Dual-axis scrolling design that makes deliberate use of both horizontal and vertical scrolling within sections. Horizontal carousels, vertical feeds, and parallax effects on both axes. Sections flow vertically but contain horizontally-scrollable content, creating a rich spatial navigation feel. Screen real estate is maximized by using both scroll dimensions.

passVariants.direction: Vertical page flow with horizontally-scrollable content rows. Dashboard has horizontal stat cards row, vertical activity feed, horizontal project carousel. Each section is a full-width band containing horizontally-scrollable content. Navigation is a vertical left rail. Parallax effects on both axes — vertical scroll moves backgrounds, horizontal scroll within sections creates depth. Maximum content density through dual-axis utilization.
